Repo 1 — html-css-mini-projects
1.	Scroll Reveal Animations Essentials Mini Project Workshop
Assignment :  Assignment: Build Scroll Reveal Animations Using HTML & CSS
Your task is to create a simple webpage where elements appear with animations as the user scrolls.
You will use HTML and CSS only to simulate scroll-based reveal effects.
________________________________________
🎯 Project Requirements
1️⃣ Page Structure (HTML)
Create a webpage with:
•	A header section (title + short description)
•	At least 3 content sections
•	Each section should include:
•	A heading
•	A paragraph of text
•	(Optional) an image or box
________________________________________
2️⃣ Scroll Reveal Animation (CSS)
•	Use CSS to create reveal-style animations, such as:
•	Fade in
•	Slide in from left / right / bottom
•	Animations should trigger as the section comes into view
(basic CSS animation approach is acceptable)
________________________________________
3️⃣ Styling & Layout
•	Use a simple color theme
•	Add spacing using margin & padding
•	Use flexbox or basic layout for alignment
•	Ensure text is readable
________________________________________
4️⃣ Responsiveness (Basic)
•	Page should be usable on:
•	Desktop
•	Mobile
•	Sections should stack properly on smaller screens
________________________________________
❌ Restrictions
•	No JavaScript
•	No animation libraries
•	Only HTML & CSS
________________________________________
📤 Submission Instructions
•	Submit HTML and CSS code only
•	No screenshots
•	No explanation-only submissions

Code: <!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"/>
  <title>Scroll Reveal - CSE Student Page</title>
  <style>
    * {
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    body {
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #f0f4f8;
      color: #333;
    }

    /* ---- HEADER ---- */
    header {
      background-color: #1a73e8;
      color: white;
      text-align: center;
      padding: 60px 20px;
    }

    header h1 {
      font-size: 2.5rem;
      margin-bottom: 12px;
    }

    header p {
      font-size: 1.1rem;
      max-width: 600px;
      margin: 0 auto;
      opacity: 0.9;
    }

    /* ---- SECTION COMMON ---- */
    .section {
      max-width: 900px;
      margin: 60px auto;
      padding: 0 20px;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 40px;
    }

    .section.reverse {
      flex-direction: row-reverse;
    }

    .section-text {
      flex: 1;
    }

    .section-text h2 {
      font-size: 1.8rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 12px;
    }

    .section-text p {
      font-size: 1rem;
      line-height: 1.7;
      color: #555;
    }

    .section-box {
      flex: 0 0 220px;
      height: 160px;
      border-radius: 12px;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: center;
      font-size: 2.5rem;
    }

    /* ---- BOX COLORS ---- */
    .box-blue   { background-color: #d0e8ff; }
    .box-green  { background-color: #d4edda; }
    .box-yellow { background-color: #fff3cd; }
    .box-pink   { background-color: #fde2e4; }

    /* ---- DIVIDER ---- */
    hr {
      max-width: 900px;
      margin: 0 auto;
      border: none;
      border-top: 1px solid #ccc;
    }

    /* ---- FOOTER ---- */
    footer {
      background-color: #1a73e8;
      color: white;
      text-align: center;
      padding: 24px;
      margin-top: 60px;
      font-size: 0.9rem;
    }

    /* ============================================
       SCROLL REVEAL ANIMATIONS (CSS ONLY)
       Using animation-timeline: view() — no JS!
    ============================================ */

    /* Fade in from bottom */
    .reveal-up {
      animation: fadeUp 0.7s ease both;
      animation-timeline: view();
      animation-range: entry 0% entry 35%;
    }

    /* Slide in from left */
    .reveal-left {
      animation: slideLeft 0.7s ease both;
      animation-timeline: view();
      animation-range: entry 0% entry 35%;
    }

    /* Slide in from right */
    .reveal-right {
      animation: slideRight 0.7s ease both;
      animation-timeline: view();
      animation-range: entry 0% entry 35%;
    }

    /* Fade in */
    .reveal-fade {
      animation: fadeIn 0.8s ease both;
      animation-timeline: view();
      animation-range: entry 0% entry 35%;
    }

    @keyframes fadeUp {
      from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(50px); }
      to   { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
    }

    @keyframes slideLeft {
      from { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-70px); }
      to   { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); }
    }

    @keyframes slideRight {
      from { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(70px); }
      to   { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); }
    }

    @keyframes fadeIn {
      from { opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.9); }
      to   { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }
    }

    /* ---- RESPONSIVE ---- */
    @media (max-width: 650px) {
      .section,
      .section.reverse {
        flex-direction: column;
        gap: 20px;
      }

      .section-box {
        width: 100%;
        flex: none;
      }

      header h1 {
        font-size: 1.8rem;
      }
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body>

  <!-- HEADER -->
  <header>
    <h1>Computer Science & Engineering</h1>
    <p>Welcome to my CSE learning page! Here I share topics I study in my B.Tech program — scroll down to explore.</p>
  </header>

  <!-- SECTION 1 - Data Structures -->
  <section class="section">
    <div class="section-text reveal-left">
      <h2>📚 Data Structures</h2>
      <p>
        Data Structures is one of the most important subjects in CSE. We learn about arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs. Understanding how to store and manage data efficiently is the foundation of good programming. I personally found binary trees the most interesting to implement!
      </p>
    </div>
    <div class="section-box box-blue reveal-right">💻</div>
  </section>

  <hr/>

  <!-- SECTION 2 - Operating Systems -->
  <section class="section reverse">
    <div class="section-text reveal-right">
      <h2>⚙️ Operating Systems</h2>
      <p>
        OS taught me how computers actually work behind the scenes. Topics like process scheduling, memory management, deadlocks, and file systems were really eye-opening. Writing a simple shell program in C during the lab was honestly one of the coolest things I've done in college so far.
      </p>
    </div>
    <div class="section-box box-green reveal-left">🖥️</div>
  </section>

  <hr/>

  <!-- SECTION 3 - Computer Networks -->
  <section class="section">
    <div class="section-text reveal-left">
      <h2>🌐 Computer Networks</h2>
      <p>
        In Networks, we study how data travels across the internet — from the physical layer all the way to the application layer. Topics like TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP, and routing protocols were very useful. After this subject I finally understood what actually happens when you type a URL in your browser!
      </p>
    </div>
    <div class="section-box box-yellow reveal-right">📡</div>
  </section>

  <hr/>

  <!-- SECTION 4 - Web Development -->
  <section class="section reverse">
    <div class="section-text reveal-up">
      <h2>🚀 Web Development</h2>
      <p>
        Web Dev is something I started learning on my own alongside college. HTML, CSS, and JavaScript are the basics — and now I am exploring React and backend with Node.js. Building projects from scratch and seeing them live on the browser is super satisfying. This page itself is one of my practice assignments!
      </p>
    </div>
    <div class="section-box box-pink reveal-fade">🌍</div>
  </section>

  <!-- FOOTER -->
  <footer>
    <p>Made by a Ashish Cherian &nbsp;|&nbsp; B.Tech Final Year &nbsp;|&nbsp; HTML & CSS Assignment</p>
  </footer>

</body>
</html>

Gemin Summary:
 This workshop provides a step-by-step guide to creating reveal on scroll animations using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The instructor focuses on using the Intersection Observer API for modern, efficient animation triggering.
Key Takeaways:
•	Project Setup (3:43): Building the basic HTML structure with a container and multiple card sections.
•	Styling (8:09): Using CSS to create a clean layout, including margin resets, padding, font styling, and applying box-shadows and border-radius to the cards.
•	Animation CSS (13:06): Defining the initial state (hidden, shifted down) and the active state (visible, original position) for the cards using transform and transition properties.
•	JavaScript Implementation (18:15):
•	Selecting all elements with the reveal class (19:23).
•	Setting up the Intersection Observer API to watch when elements enter the viewport (21:19).
•	Defining a callback function to add an active class to elements when they become visible (24:28).
•	Using unobserve to ensure the animation only plays once (29:34).
•	Final Testing (31:56): Demonstrating the smooth, triggered fade-in and slide-up effect as you scroll through the page.

Certificate and details: 
Scroll Reveal Animations Essentials Mini Project Workshop
Create smooth scroll-triggered animations for web pages using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to make websites more interactive and engaging.
Training guided by an experienced developer
Power-packed 1-hour session
 Build scroll-triggered interactive animations
Get Certification from NSDC & GDG MAD

 






2.	Product Card Essentials Mini Project Workshop
Assignment :  
Assignment Objective
Build a modern product card UI component using HTML and CSS only.
🛍️ Task: Design a Modern Product Card
You must create a single Product Card UI that includes:
________________________________________
📌 Required Elements
1️⃣ Product Image
•	Display a product image at the top
•	Image should fit properly inside the card
________________________________________
2️⃣ Product Title
Example:
Wireless Headphones
•	Use proper heading tag (h2 or h3)
________________________________________
3️⃣ Product Description
•	1–2 lines describing the product
Example:
High-quality wireless headphones with noise cancellation.
________________________________________
4️⃣ Product Price
Example:
$99
•	Make price visually prominent
________________________________________
5️⃣ Call-to-Action Button
Button text:
Add to Cart
•	Styled button
•	Hover effect required
________________________________________
🎨 Styling Requirements
•	Card must have:
•	Padding
•	Border-radius
•	Box shadow
•	Proper spacing between elements
•	Center card on page
•	Clean modern color theme
•	Button must have hover effect
•	Use flexbox OR basic layout
________________________________________
📱 Responsiveness (Basic)
•	Card should not break on smaller screens
•	Image should scale properly
•	Content should remain readable
________________________________________
❌ Restrictions
•	No JavaScript
•	No frameworks (Bootstrap, Tailwind, etc.)
•	Only HTML & CSS
•	No copied templates
________________________________________
📤 Submission Requirements
Students must submit:
•	Submit complete HTML code in text format
•	CSS file (or internal CSS)
•	No screenshots only
•	Must include hover effect

Code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"/>
  <title>Product Card - CSE Final Year</title>
  <style>

    /* basic reset */
    * {
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    body {
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #f2f2f2;
      min-height: 100vh;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: center;
      padding: 20px;
    }

    /* the card itself */
    .card {
      background-color: #ffffff;
      width: 100%;
      max-width: 340px;
      border-radius: 12px;
      box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12);
      overflow: hidden;
      transition: transform 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease;
    }

    /* slight lift on hover - nice touch */
    .card:hover {
      transform: translateY(-6px);
      box-shadow: 0 10px 28px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.18);
    }

    /* product image */
    .card img {
      width: 100%;
      height: 220px;
      object-fit: cover;
      display: block;
    }

    /* all text content below the image */
    .card-body {
      padding: 20px;
    }

    /* badge like "New Arrival" - optional but looks good */
    .badge {
      display: inline-block;
      background-color: #e8f0fe;
      color: #1a73e8;
      font-size: 0.72rem;
      padding: 3px 10px;
      border-radius: 20px;
      margin-bottom: 10px;
      letter-spacing: 0.5px;
    }

    /* product title */
    .card-body h2 {
      font-size: 1.25rem;
      color: #222;
      margin-bottom: 8px;
    }

    /* product description */
    .card-body p {
      font-size: 0.92rem;
      color: #666;
      line-height: 1.6;
      margin-bottom: 16px;
    }

    /* price + rating row */
    .card-meta {
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: space-between;
      margin-bottom: 18px;
    }

    .price {
      font-size: 1.5rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      color: #1a73e8;
    }

    .rating {
      font-size: 0.85rem;
      color: #f5a623;
    }

    /* add to cart button */
    .btn {
      display: block;
      width: 100%;
      padding: 12px;
      background-color: #1a73e8;
      color: white;
      font-size: 1rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      border: none;
      border-radius: 8px;
      cursor: pointer;
      text-align: center;
      text-decoration: none;
      transition: background-color 0.3s ease, transform 0.2s ease;
    }

    /* hover effect on button - required by assignment */
    .btn:hover {
      background-color: #1558b0;
      transform: scale(1.03);
    }

    /* responsive - for small screens */
    @media (max-width: 400px) {
      .card-body h2 {
        font-size: 1.1rem;
      }

      .price {
        font-size: 1.3rem;
      }

      .btn {
        font-size: 0.95rem;
      }
    }

  </style>
</head>
<body>

  <div class="card">

    <!-- product image - using a placeholder image -->
    <img
      src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1505740420928-5e560c06d30e?w=400&h=220&fit=crop"
      alt="Wireless Headphones"
    />

    <div class="card-body">

      <!-- small badge -->
      <span class="badge">New Arrival</span>

      <!-- product title -->
      <h2>Wireless Headphones</h2>

      <!-- product description -->
      <p>High-quality wireless headphones with noise cancellation and 30-hour battery life.</p>

      <!-- price and rating -->
      <div class="card-meta">
        <span class="price">$99</span>
        <span class="rating">★★★★☆ (4.2)</span>
      </div>

      <!-- call to action button -->
      <a href="#" class="btn">Add to Cart</a>

    </div>
  </div>

</body>
</html>

Cerificate details:
Product Card Essentials Mini Project Workshop
Design and build a modern, responsive product card using HTML, CSS, and light JavaScript for real-world UI use.
Training guided by an experienced developer
Power-packed 1-hour session
 Build real e-commerce UI components
Get Certification from NSDC & GDG MAD
 










3.	
Assignment :  
Assignment: Simple Landing Page for a Product / Event
🎯 Objective
Create a simple landing page using only HTML and CSS that introduces a product, event, or service.
________________________________________
Task: Build a Simple Landing Page
Create a static landing page using HTML and CSS only.
________________________________________
1️⃣ HTML Structure Requirements
Your page must include:
•	Header
•	Page title (Product / Event / App name)
•	Short tagline or description
•	Navigation Bar
•	Links: Home, Features, Contact (links don’t need to work)
•	Hero Section
•	One heading
•	One paragraph describing the product/event
•	One button (e.g., “Get Started” or “Register Now”)
•	Features Section
•	At least 3 features, each with:
•	Feature title
•	Short description
•	Footer
•	Simple copyright text
________________________________________
2️⃣ CSS Styling Requirements
•	Use a consistent color theme
•	Style headings, paragraphs, and buttons
•	Add spacing using margin and padding
•	Align content neatly
•	Make the page readable and clean
________________________________________
3️⃣ Layout & Responsiveness (Basic)
•	Use flexbox for layout where applicable
•	Page should look acceptable on:
•	Mobile
•	Desktop
•	Sections should stack naturally on smaller screens
________________________________________
📤 Submission Instructions
•	Cppy and Paste the HTML and CSS code only in text box
Code: <!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"/>
  <title>StudyBuddy - Your Study Companion</title>
  <style>

    /* reset */
    * {
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    body {
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #f9f9f9;
      color: #333;
    }

    /* =====================
       HEADER
    ===================== */
    header {
      background-color: #1a73e8;
      color: white;
      text-align: center;
      padding: 30px 20px 20px;
    }

    header h1 {
      font-size: 2.2rem;
      margin-bottom: 8px;
    }

    header p {
      font-size: 1rem;
      opacity: 0.88;
    }

    /* =====================
       NAVIGATION BAR
    ===================== */
    nav {
      background-color: #1558b0;
      display: flex;
      justify-content: center;
      gap: 30px;
      padding: 12px 20px;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
    }

    nav a {
      color: white;
      text-decoration: none;
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      padding: 5px 10px;
      border-radius: 4px;
      transition: background-color 0.3s ease;
    }

    nav a:hover {
      background-color: #ffffff33;
    }

    /* =====================
       HERO SECTION
    ===================== */
    .hero {
      background-color: #e8f0fe;
      text-align: center;
      padding: 70px 20px;
    }

    .hero h2 {
      font-size: 2rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 16px;
    }

    .hero p {
      font-size: 1.05rem;

Cerificate details:
HTML & CSS Bootcamp
HTML and CSS are vital for creating responsive, attractive websites and offer numerous front-end development job opportunities
100% Live Training by Web Development Experts
Program Duration - 3 Days, 1.5 hours each day
Build Responsive Websites and User Interfaces
Get Certification from NSDC, ITM Group & GDG MAD








4.	Survey Form Essentials: HTML & CSS Mini Project

Assignment :  
Assignment: Build a Simple Survey Form
Create a static survey form using only HTML and CSS.
The form should collect basic user feedback and be visually clean and readable.
________________________________________
1️⃣ HTML Requirements
Your survey form must include:
•	Page Title
•	Heading: “User Feedback Survey” (or similar)
•	Form Fields
•	Full Name (text input)
•	Email Address (email input)
•	Age (number input)
•	Gender (radio buttons)
•	Topics of Interest (checkboxes – at least 3)
•	Feedback / Comments (textarea)
•	Submit button
•	Use proper HTML form elements:
•	<form>, <label>, <input>, <textarea>, <button>
________________________________________
2️⃣ CSS Styling Requirements
•	Use a simple color theme
•	Align form neatly (centered or contained layout)
•	Add spacing using margin and padding
•	Style labels, inputs, and buttons
•	Make the form readable and clean
________________________________________
3️⃣ Layout & Responsiveness (Basic)
•	Form should look usable on:
•	Mobile
•	Desktop
•	Inputs should stretch properly on smaller screens
________________________________________
📤 Submission Instructions
•	Submit HTML and CSS code only
•	No screenshots
•	No JavaScript required
•	No explanations without code


Code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"/>
  <title>User Feedback Survey</title>
  <style>

    /* basic reset */
    * {
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    body {
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #f0f4f8;
      color: #333;
      padding: 40px 16px;
    }

    /* page heading */
    .page-title {
      text-align: center;
      margin-bottom: 30px;
    }

    .page-title h1 {
      font-size: 1.9rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 6px;
    }

    .page-title p {
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      color: #666;
    }

    /* form card */
    form {
      background-color: #ffffff;
      max-width: 600px;
      margin: 0 auto;
      padding: 36px 32px;
      border-radius: 12px;
      box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
    }

    /* each field group */
    .form-group {
      margin-bottom: 20px;
    }

    label {
      display: block;
      font-size: 0.9rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      color: #444;
      margin-bottom: 7px;
    }

    /* text, email, number inputs */
    input[type="text"],
    input[type="email"],
    input[type="number"],
    textarea {
      width: 100%;
      padding: 10px 14px;
      border: 1px solid #ccc;
      border-radius: 8px;
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #f9f9f9;
      color: #333;
      transition: border-color 0.2s ease;
    }

    input[type="text"]:focus,
    input[type="email"]:focus,
    input[type="number"]:focus,
    textarea:focus {
      border-color: #1a73e8;
      outline: none;
      background-color: #fff;
    }

    textarea {
      height: 110px;
      resize: vertical;
    }

    /* radio and checkbox rows */
    .options-group {
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
      gap: 9px;
    }

    .options-group label {
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 9px;
      font-weight: normal;
      font-size: 0.92rem;
      cursor: pointer;
      color: #555;
    }

    input[type="radio"],
    input[type="checkbox"] {
      width: 16px;
      height: 16px;
      accent-color: #1a73e8;
      cursor: pointer;
    }

    /* divider between sections */
    .divider {
      border: none;
      border-top: 1px solid #e8e8e8;
      margin: 24px 0;
    }

    /* section mini heading */
    .section-label {
      font-size: 0.78rem;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      letter-spacing: 0.6px;
      color: #1a73e8;
      font-weight: bold;
      margin-bottom: 14px;
    }

    /* submit button */
    .btn-submit {
      display: block;
      width: 100%;
      padding: 13px;
      background-color: #1a73e8;
      color: white;
      font-size: 1rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      border: none;
      border-radius: 8px;
      cursor: pointer;
      margin-top: 8px;
      transition: background-color 0.3s ease, transform 0.2s ease;
    }

    .btn-submit:hover {
      background-color: #1558b0;
      transform: scale(1.02);
    }

    /* required star */
    .required {
      color: #e53935;
      margin-left: 3px;
    }

    /* responsive */
    @media (max-width: 500px) {
      form {
        padding: 24px 18px;
      }

      .page-title h1 {
        font-size: 1.5rem;
      }
    }

  </style>
</head>
<body>

  <!-- PAGE TITLE -->
  <div class="page-title">
    <h1>📋 User Feedback Survey</h1>
    <p>We value your feedback! Please fill out the form below.</p>
  </div>

  <!-- SURVEY FORM -->
  <form>

    <!-- PERSONAL INFO -->
    <p class="section-label">Personal Information</p>

    <div class="form-group">
      <label for="fullname">Full Name <span class="required">*</span></label>
      <input type="text" id="fullname" name="fullname" placeholder="e.g. Ashish Cherian" />
    </div>

    <div class="form-group">
      <label for="email">Email Address <span class="required">*</span></label>
      <input type="email" id="email" name="email" placeholder="e.g. ashishcherian15@gmail.com" />
    </div>

    <div class="form-group">
      <label for="age">Age</label>
      <input type="number" id="age" name="age" placeholder="e.g. 21" min="1" max="100" />
    </div>

    <hr class="divider" />

    <!-- GENDER -->
    <p class="section-label">Gender</p>
    <div class="form-group">
      <div class="options-group">
        <label><input type="radio" name="gender" value="male" /> Male</label>
        <label><input type="radio" name="gender" value="female" /> Female</label>
        <label><input type="radio" name="gender" value="other" /> Prefer not to say</label>
      </div>
    </div>

    <hr class="divider" />

    <!-- TOPICS OF INTEREST -->
    <p class="section-label">Topics of Interest</p>
    <div class="form-group">
      <div class="options-group">
        <label><input type="checkbox" name="topic" value="webdev" /> Web Development</label>
        <label><input type="checkbox" name="topic" value="ml" /> Machine Learning / AI</label>
        <label><input type="checkbox" name="topic" value="dsa" /> Data Structures & Algorithms</label>
        <label><input type="checkbox" name="topic" value="cloud" /> Cloud Computing</label>
        <label><input type="checkbox" name="topic" value="cyber" /> Cybersecurity</label>
      </div>
    </div>

    <hr class="divider" />

    <!-- FEEDBACK -->
    <p class="section-label">Your Feedback</p>
    <div class="form-group">
      <label for="comments">Comments / Suggestions</label>
      <textarea id="comments" name="comments" placeholder="Write your feedback here..."></textarea>
    </div>

    <!-- SUBMIT -->
    <button type="submit" class="btn-submit">Submit Feedback</button>

  </form>

</body>
</html>

5.	Testimonial Block Essentials Mini Project in HTML & CSS
Assignment :  
Assignment: Build a Testimonial Section Using HTML & CSS
Your task is to design a testimonial section for a website using only HTML and CSS.
Requirements
1️⃣ Structure (HTML)
Create a testimonial block that includes:
•	A section heading (e.g., “What Our Users Say”)
•	At least 3 testimonials
•	Each testimonial must include:
•	User name
•	Short testimonial text
•	User role or company (e.g., “Frontend Developer”, “Student”)
2️⃣ Styling (CSS)
•	Use CSS to visually separate each testimonial (cards, borders, or background)
•	Apply spacing (padding & margins) for readability
•	Style text hierarchy (heading > name > role > testimonial)
•	Align testimonials neatly (flexbox or basic layout)
3️⃣ Layout & Responsiveness
•	Testimonials should align side-by-side on larger screens
•	Stack vertically on smaller screens (mobile-friendly)
📤 Submission Instructions
Submit only your complete HTML and CSS code in the text box provided.




Code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"/>
  <title>Testimonial Section</title>
  <style>

    /* basic reset */
    * {
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    body {
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #f0f4f8;
      color: #333;
      padding: 50px 16px;
    }

    /* section heading area */
    .section-header {
      text-align: center;
      margin-bottom: 40px;
    }

    .section-header h2 {
      font-size: 1.9rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 8px;
    }

    .section-header p {
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      color: #777;
    }

    /* testimonials row using flexbox */
    .testimonials-container {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 22px;
      justify-content: center;
      max-width: 1050px;
      margin: 0 auto;
    }

    /* individual testimonial card */
    .card {
      background-color: #ffffff;
      border-radius: 12px;
      box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.09);
      padding: 28px 24px;
      flex: 1 1 280px;
      max-width: 320px;
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
      gap: 14px;
      transition: transform 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease;
      border-top: 4px solid #1a73e8;
    }

    .card:hover {
      transform: translateY(-5px);
      box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.13);
    }

    /* big quote mark at top */
    .quote-mark {
      font-size: 3rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      line-height: 1;
      font-family: Georgia, serif;
    }

    /* testimonial text */
    .card-text {
      font-size: 0.93rem;
      color: #555;
      line-height: 1.7;
      flex-grow: 1;
    }

    /* star rating */
    .stars {
      color: #f5a623;
      font-size: 1rem;
      letter-spacing: 2px;
    }

    /* bottom user info */
    .card-footer {
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 13px;
      border-top: 1px solid #eee;
      padding-top: 14px;
    }

    /* avatar circle with initials */
    .avatar {
      width: 46px;
      height: 46px;
      border-radius: 50%;
      background-color: #e8f0fe;
      color: #1a73e8;
      font-weight: bold;
      font-size: 1rem;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: center;
      flex-shrink: 0;
    }

    .user-info {
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
      gap: 2px;
    }

    /* user name */
    .user-name {
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      color: #222;
    }

    /* user role */
    .user-role {
      font-size: 0.8rem;
      color: #888;
    }

    /* slightly different accent colors for each card */
    .card:nth-child(2) { border-top-color: #27ae60; }
    .card:nth-child(2) .quote-mark { color: #27ae60; }

6.	
Assignment :  
Assignment Title
Build a Personal Profile Webpage
________________________________________
📌 Objective
Create a simple personal profile webpage using only HTML and CSS.
This assignment tests basic understanding of:
•	HTML structure
•	CSS styling
•	Layout
•	Text formatting
•	Image usage
________________________________________
📝 Assignment Task
Create a single webpage that includes the following sections.
________________________________________
1️⃣ Header Section
Include:
•	Your name as a heading
•	A short tagline or role
Example:
Aspiring Web Developer
________________________________________
2️⃣ About Me Section
Write 3–4 sentences about yourself.
Example topics:
•	Your interests
•	What you are learning
•	Your goals in tech
________________________________________
3️⃣ Skills Section
Create a list of at least 4 skills using:
•	<ul> or <ol>
Example:
•	HTML
•	CSS
•	JavaScript
•	Problem Solving
________________________________________
4️⃣ Profile Image
Add one image of:
•	yourself
•	or a placeholder image
Use the <img> tag.
________________________________________
5️⃣ Contact Section
Add:
•	Email
•	City
•	One social media link
________________________________________
🎨 CSS Styling Requirements
Students must create basic CSS styling:
•	Background color
•	Font styling
•	Padding and margins
•	Center the main heading
•	Add a border or card style to one section
CSS can be:
•	Internal <style> OR
•	External style.css
________________________________________
📱 Basic Layout
Page should:
•	Have proper spacing
•	Be readable
•	Not look cluttered
No frameworks allowed (Bootstrap, Tailwind etc.).
________________________________________
📤 Submission Format
Students must submit their HTML and CSS code in the text box.
No screenshots.


Code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"/>
  <title>Ashish - Personal Profile</title>
  <style>

    /* basic reset */
    * {
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    body {
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #f0f4f8;
      color: #333;
      padding: 30px 16px;
    }

    /* wrapper to keep content centered */
    .wrapper {
      max-width: 720px;
      margin: 0 auto;
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
      gap: 24px;
    }

    /* ── HEADER ── */
    header {
      background-color: #1a73e8;
      color: white;
      text-align: center;
      padding: 40px 24px;
      border-radius: 12px;
    }

    header h1 {
      font-size: 2rem;
      margin-bottom: 8px;
    }

    header p {
      font-size: 1rem;
      opacity: 0.9;
    }

    /* shared card style */
    .card {
      background-color: #ffffff;
      border-radius: 12px;
      padding: 26px 24px;
      box-shadow: 0 3px 12px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08);
    }

    .card h2 {
      font-size: 1.2rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 14px;
      border-bottom: 2px solid #e8f0fe;
      padding-bottom: 8px;
    }

    /* ── PROFILE IMAGE + ABOUT — side by side ── */
    .about-section {
      display: flex;
      gap: 24px;
      align-items: flex-start;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
    }

    .profile-image-box {
      flex-shrink: 0;
      text-align: center;
    }

    .profile-image-box img {
      width: 130px;
      height: 130px;
      border-radius: 50%;
      object-fit: cover;
      border: 4px solid #1a73e8;
      display: block;
    }

    .profile-image-box p {
      font-size: 0.75rem;
      color: #aaa;
      margin-top: 6px;
    }

    .about-text p {
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      line-height: 1.75;
      color: #555;
    }

    /* ── SKILLS ── */
    .skills-list {
      list-style: none;
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 10px;
    }

    .skills-list li {
      background-color: #e8f0fe;
      color: #1a73e8;
      padding: 7px 18px;
      border-radius: 20px;
      font-size: 0.88rem;
      font-weight: bold;
    }

    /* ── CONTACT — card with border ── */
    .contact-card {
      background-color: #ffffff;
      border-radius: 12px;
      padding: 26px 24px;
      box-shadow: 0 3px 12px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08);
      border: 2px solid #1a73e8;   /* border as required */
    }

    .contact-card h2 {
      font-size: 1.2rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 16px;
      border-bottom: 2px solid #e8f0fe;
      padding-bottom: 8px;
    }

    .contact-item {
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 12px;
      margin-bottom: 12px;
      font-size: 0.93rem;
      color: #444;
    }

    .contact-item span.icon {
      font-size: 1.1rem;
      width: 28px;
      text-align: center;
    }

    .contact-item a {
      color: #1a73e8;
      text-decoration: none;
    }

    .contact-item a:hover {
      text-decoration: underline;
    }

    /* ── FOOTER ── */
    footer {
      text-align: center;
      font-size: 0.82rem;
      color: #aaa;
      padding: 10px 0;
    }

    /* responsive */
    @media (max-width: 500px) {
      header h1        { font-size: 1.5rem; }
      .about-section   { flex-direction: column; align-items: center; }
      .about-text      { text-align: center; }
    }

  </style>
</head>
<body>

  <div class="wrapper">

    <!-- HEADER -->
    <header>
      <h1>Ashish M</h1>
      <p>Aspiring Software Developer | CSE Final Year Student</p>
    </header>

    <!-- ABOUT + PROFILE IMAGE -->
    <div class="card">
      <h2>👤 About Me</h2>
      <div class="about-section">

        <!-- profile image -->
        <div class="profile-image-box">
          <img
            src="https://ui-avatars.com/api/?name=Ashish+M&background=1a73e8&color=fff&size=130"
            alt="Ashish Profile Photo"
          />
          <p>Ashish cherian</p>
        </div>

        <!-- about text -->
        <div class="about-text">
          <p>
            Hi! I am Ashish cherian, a final year B.E student in Computer Science and
            Engineering at Karavali Institute of Technology, Mangalore. I am passionate about web development,
            problem solving, and building projects that solve real-world problems.
            I enjoy learning new technologies and I am currently exploring React,
            Python, and Machine Learning. My goal is to become a full stack
            developer and work on impactful products after graduation.
          </p>
        </div>

      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- SKILLS -->
    <div class="card">
      <h2>🛠️ Skills</h2>
      <ul class="skills-list">
        <li>HTML</li>
        <li>CSS</li>
        <li>JavaScript</li>
        <li>React</li>
        <li>Python</li>
        <li>Problem Solving</li>
        <li>Git & GitHub</li>
        <li>Android (Jetpack Compose)</li>
      </ul>
    </div>

    <!-- CONTACT — card with border -->
    <div class="contact-card">
      <h2>📬 Contact</h2>

      <div class="contact-item">
        <span class="icon">📧</span>
        <span>ashishcherian15@gmail.com</span>
      </div>

      <div class="contact-item">
        <span class="icon">📍</span>
        <span>Mangalore, Karnataka, India</span>
      </div>

      <div class="contact-item">
        <span class="icon">💼</span>
        <a href="https://linkedin.com/in/ashish" target="_blank">
          linkedin.com/in/ashish
        </a>
      </div>

    </div>

    <!-- FOOTER -->
    <footer>
      <p>&copy; 2026 Ashish Cherian &nbsp;|&nbsp; Personal Profile Page</p>
    </footer>

  </div>

</body>
</html>

Repo 2 — javascript-mini-projects

1.	Make an Interactive "Guess the Number" Game
Assignment :  
Assignment: Build an Interactive “Guess the Number” Game
Your task is to build a simple Guess the Number game using JavaScript.
🎯 Game Rules
•	The system should generate a random number between 1 and 100
•	The user should:
•	Enter a number
•	Click a “Check Guess” button
•	The game should display feedback:
•	“Too High”
•	“Too Low”
•	“Correct Guess 🎉”
📌 Functional Requirements
1️⃣ User Input
•	Input field to enter a number
•	Button to submit the guess
2️⃣ Game Logic
•	Random number generated using JavaScript
•	Compare user guess with the generated number
•	Show appropriate feedback message
3️⃣ Attempts Tracking
•	Display the number of attempts taken by the user
4️⃣ Game Reset
•	Provide a Reset / Play Again button
•	Generate a new random number on reset
5️⃣ Basic UI
•	Clean and readable layout
•	Can be text-based or lightly styled with CSS
📤 Submission Instructions
Submit your complete HTML, CSS, and JavaScript code in the text box provided.



Code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"/>
  <title>Guess the Number Game</title>
  <style>

    /* basic reset */
    * {
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    body {
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #f0f4f8;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: center;
      min-height: 100vh;
      padding: 20px;
    }

    /* main game card */
    .game-card {
      background-color: #ffffff;
      border-radius: 14px;
      box-shadow: 0 4px 18px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
      padding: 36px 32px;
      max-width: 440px;
      width: 100%;
      text-align: center;
    }

    /* title */
    .game-card h1 {
      font-size: 1.7rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 6px;
    }

    .game-card .subtitle {
      font-size: 0.9rem;
      color: #888;
      margin-bottom: 28px;
    }

    /* input field */
    input[type="number"] {
      width: 100%;
      padding: 11px 14px;
      font-size: 1rem;
      border: 1px solid #ccc;
      border-radius: 8px;
      background-color: #f9f9f9;
      color: #333;
      text-align: center;
      margin-bottom: 14px;
      outline: none;
      transition: border-color 0.2s;
    }

    input[type="number"]:focus {
      border-color: #1a73e8;
      background-color: #fff;
    }

    /* buttons */
    .btn {
      width: 100%;
      padding: 12px;
      font-size: 1rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      border: none;
      border-radius: 8px;
      cursor: pointer;
      transition: background-color 0.3s ease, transform 0.2s ease;
      margin-bottom: 10px;
    }

    .btn-check {
      background-color: #1a73e8;
      color: white;
    }

    .btn-check:hover {
      background-color: #1558b0;
      transform: scale(1.02);
    }

    .btn-reset {
      background-color: #e8f0fe;
      color: #1a73e8;
    }

    .btn-reset:hover {
      background-color: #d0e2ff;
      transform: scale(1.02);
    }

    /* feedback message box */
    .message-box {
      margin: 18px 0;
      padding: 14px;
      border-radius: 8px;
      font-size: 1rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      min-height: 50px;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: center;
    }

    /* message colors */
    .msg-high    { background-color: #fdecea; color: #c0392b; }
    .msg-low     { background-color: #fff8e1; color: #e67e22; }
    .msg-correct { background-color: #e6f4ea; color: #27ae60; }
    .msg-default { background-color: #f0f4f8; color: #888; }

    /* attempts counter */
    .attempts {
      font-size: 0.88rem;
      color: #666;
      margin-bottom: 6px;
    }

    .attempts span {
      font-weight: bold;
      color: #1a73e8;
    }

    /* hint range text */
    .range-hint {
      font-size: 0.82rem;
      color: #aaa;
      margin-bottom: 18px;
    }

    /* history list */
    .history-title {
      font-size: 0.8rem;
      color: #aaa;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      letter-spacing: 0.5px;
      margin-top: 18px;
      margin-bottom: 8px;
    }

    .history-list {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 7px;
      justify-content: center;
    }

    .history-tag {
      background-color: #f0f4f8;
      border-radius: 20px;
      padding: 3px 12px;
      font-size: 0.82rem;
      color: #555;
    }

    /* responsive */
    @media (max-width: 480px) {
      .game-card {
        padding: 24px 18px;
      }
      .game-card h1 {
        font-size: 1.4rem;
      }
    }

  </style>
</head>
<body>

  <div class="game-card">
    <h1>🎯 Guess the Number</h1>
    <p class="subtitle">I'm thinking of a number between 1 and 100</p>

    <!-- range hint updates as guesses narrow it down -->
    <p class="range-hint" id="rangeHint">Range: 1 – 100</p>

    <!-- input -->
    <input
      type="number"
      id="guessInput"
      placeholder="Enter your guess..."
      min="1"
      max="100"
    />

    <!-- check button -->
    <button class="btn btn-check" onclick="checkGuess()">Check Guess</button>

    <!-- feedback message -->
    <div class="message-box msg-default" id="messageBox">
      Enter a number and click Check Guess!
    </div>

    <!-- attempts counter -->
    <p class="attempts">Attempts: <span id="attemptCount">0</span></p>

    <!-- reset button -->
    <button class="btn btn-reset" onclick="resetGame()">🔄 Play Again</button>

    <!-- guess history -->
    <p class="history-title" id="historyTitle"></p>
    <div class="history-list" id="historyList"></div>
  </div>

  <script>

    // generate random number between 1 and 100
    let secretNumber = Math.floor(Math.random() * 100) + 1;
    let attempts     = 0;
    let gameOver     = false;
    let lowBound     = 1;
    let highBound    = 100;
    let guessHistory = [];

    function checkGuess() {
      // if game already won, do nothing
      if (gameOver) return;

      const input     = document.getElementById("guessInput");
      const msgBox    = document.getElementById("messageBox");
      const countEl   = document.getElementById("attemptCount");
      const rangeEl   = document.getElementById("rangeHint");
      const userGuess = parseInt(input.value);

      // validate input
      if (isNaN(userGuess) || userGuess < 1 || userGuess > 100) {
        msgBox.textContent = "⚠️ Please enter a valid number between 1 and 100.";
        msgBox.className   = "message-box msg-default";
        return;
      }

      // count the attempt
      attempts++;
      countEl.textContent = attempts;
      guessHistory.push(userGuess);
      updateHistory();

      if (userGuess > secretNumber) {
        // too high
        msgBox.textContent = "📈 Too High! Try a lower number.";
        msgBox.className   = "message-box msg-high";
        if (userGuess < highBound) highBound = userGuess - 1;

      } else if (userGuess < secretNumber) {
        // too low
        msgBox.textContent = "📉 Too Low! Try a higher number.";
        msgBox.className   = "message-box msg-low";
        if (userGuess > lowBound) lowBound = userGuess + 1;

      } else {
        // correct!
        msgBox.textContent = "🎉 Correct Guess! You got it in " + attempts + " attempt(s)!";
        msgBox.className   = "message-box msg-correct";
        gameOver = true;
      }

      // update the range hint
      rangeEl.textContent = "Range: " + lowBound + " – " + highBound;

      // clear input
      input.value = "";
      input.focus();
    }

    function resetGame() {
      // generate a fresh number
      secretNumber = Math.floor(Math.random() * 100) + 1;
      attempts     = 0;
      gameOver     = false;
      lowBound     = 1;
      highBound    = 100;
      guessHistory = [];

      // reset UI
      document.getElementById("attemptCount").textContent = "0";
      document.getElementById("guessInput").value         = "";
      document.getElementById("rangeHint").textContent    = "Range: 1 – 100";
      document.getElementById("historyList").innerHTML    = "";
      document.getElementById("historyTitle").textContent = "";

      const msgBox      = document.getElementById("messageBox");
      msgBox.textContent = "Enter a number and click Check Guess!";
      msgBox.className   = "message-box msg-default";

      document.getElementById("guessInput").focus();
    }

    function updateHistory() {
      const list  = document.getElementById("historyList");
      const title = document.getElementById("historyTitle");
      title.textContent = "Your Guesses";
      list.innerHTML = "";
      guessHistory.forEach(function(g) {
        const tag = document.createElement("span");
        tag.className   = "history-tag";
        tag.textContent = g;
        list.appendChild(tag);
      });
    }

    // allow pressing Enter key to submit guess
    document.getElementById("guessInput").addEventListener("keydown", function(e) {
      if (e.key === "Enter") checkGuess();
    });

  </script>

</body>
</html>

2.	View Transitions API Essentials Workshop 
Assignment :  
Assignment: View Transitions API – Smooth Page Transitions

🎯 Objective
Learn how to use the View Transitions API to create smooth visual transitions between UI states or page content changes using modern JavaScript and CSS.

🛠️ Task Description
1️⃣ Create a Basic Web Page
Your page must include:
•	A main content container
•	Two different UI states or views, such as:
•	Home view & About view
•	List view & Detail view
•	Light content & Alternate content
Only one view should be visible at a time.

2️⃣ Implement View Transitions (Mandatory)
Using the View Transitions API:
•	Trigger a transition when the view changes
•	Use:
document.startViewTransition(() => {
  // DOM update
});
•	The DOM update inside the callback should:
•	Switch the visible content
•	Replace or update elements meaningfully

4️⃣ User Interaction
•	Add a button or link to trigger the transition
•	Transition should be clearly visible when clicked

5️⃣ Insight (Mandatory)
Add 2–3 lines as comments explaining:
•	What the View Transitions API does
•	Why it improves user experience compared to instant DOM changes

Code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"/>
  <title>View Transitions API – CSE Assignment</title>
  <style>

    /* basic reset */
    * {
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    body {
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #f0f4f8;
      min-height: 100vh;
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
      align-items: center;
      padding: 40px 16px;
      color: #333;
    }

    /* page heading */
    h1 {
      font-size: 1.6rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 6px;
      text-align: center;
    }

    .subtitle {
      font-size: 0.88rem;
      color: #888;
      margin-bottom: 30px;
      text-align: center;
    }

    /* nav tabs to switch views */
    .nav-tabs {
      display: flex;
      gap: 12px;
      margin-bottom: 28px;
    }

    .tab-btn {
      padding: 10px 24px;
      border: 2px solid #1a73e8;
      border-radius: 8px;
      background-color: transparent;
      color: #1a73e8;
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      cursor: pointer;
      transition: background-color 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease;
    }

    .tab-btn.active {
      background-color: #1a73e8;
      color: white;
    }

    .tab-btn:hover:not(.active) {
      background-color: #e8f0fe;
    }

    /* main content container */
    #content-box {
      background-color: #ffffff;
      border-radius: 14px;
      box-shadow: 0 4px 18px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);
      padding: 36px 32px;
      max-width: 620px;
      width: 100%;
      min-height: 340px;

      /* view-transition-name tells the browser to animate THIS element */
      view-transition-name: content-area;
    }

    /* ── VIEW TRANSITION ANIMATIONS ──────────────────────
       ::view-transition-old  = the snapshot of the OLD content
       ::view-transition-new  = the snapshot of the NEW content
       We fade old out and slide new in from the right.
    ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    ::view-transition-old(content-area) {
      animation: slideOutLeft 0.35s ease forwards;
    }

    ::view-transition-new(content-area) {
      animation: slideInRight 0.35s ease forwards;
    }

    @keyframes slideOutLeft {
      from { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); }
      to   { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-60px); }
    }

    @keyframes slideInRight {
      from { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(60px); }
      to   { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); }
    }

    /* ── HOME VIEW ─── */
    .view-home h2 {
      font-size: 1.5rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 12px;
    }

    .view-home p {
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      color: #555;
      line-height: 1.7;
      margin-bottom: 20px;
    }

    .home-cards {
      display: flex;
      gap: 14px;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
    }

    .home-card {
      flex: 1 1 140px;
      background-color: #e8f0fe;
      border-radius: 10px;
      padding: 18px 14px;
      text-align: center;
    }

    .home-card .icon { font-size: 1.8rem; margin-bottom: 8px; }
    .home-card p    { font-size: 0.85rem; color: #444; margin: 0; }

    /* ── ABOUT VIEW ─── */
    .view-about h2 {
      font-size: 1.5rem;
      color: #27ae60;
      margin-bottom: 12px;
    }

    .view-about p {
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      color: #555;
      line-height: 1.7;
      margin-bottom: 16px;
    }

    .about-list {
      list-style: none;
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
      gap: 10px;
    }

    .about-list li {
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 10px;
      background-color: #eafaf1;
      border-radius: 8px;
      padding: 12px 14px;
      font-size: 0.9rem;
      color: #333;
    }

    .about-list li span {
      font-size: 1.2rem;
    }

    /* ── CONTACT VIEW ─── */
    .view-contact h2 {
      font-size: 1.5rem;
      color: #e67e22;
      margin-bottom: 12px;
    }

    .view-contact p {
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      color: #555;
      line-height: 1.7;
      margin-bottom: 20px;
    }

    .contact-info {
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
      gap: 12px;
    }

    .contact-row {
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 12px;
      background-color: #fff3e0;
      border-radius: 8px;
      padding: 12px 16px;
      font-size: 0.9rem;
      color: #444;
    }

    .contact-row span { font-size: 1.3rem; }

    /* fallback notice */
    .fallback-notice {
      margin-top: 18px;
      background-color: #fff8e1;
      border-left: 4px solid #f5a623;
      padding: 10px 14px;
      border-radius: 6px;
      font-size: 0.82rem;
      color: #7a5c00;
      display: none;
    }

    /* responsive */
    @media (max-width: 480px) {
      #content-box { padding: 22px 16px; }
      .nav-tabs     { gap: 8px; }
      .tab-btn      { padding: 8px 14px; font-size: 0.85rem; }
    }

  </style>
</head>
<body>

  <h1>🌐 View Transitions API</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">Click the tabs below to switch views with smooth animations</p>

  <!-- NAV TABS -->
  <div class="nav-tabs">
    <button class="tab-btn active" onclick="switchView('home', this)">🏠 Home</button>
    <button class="tab-btn"       onclick="switchView('about', this)">👤 About</button>
    <button class="tab-btn"       onclick="switchView('contact', this)">📬 Contact</button>
  </div>

  <!-- MAIN CONTENT BOX — only one view shows at a time -->
  <div id="content-box">
    <!-- default: home view loads first -->
  </div>

  <p class="fallback-notice" id="fallback-notice">
    ⚠️ Your browser does not support the View Transitions API. Content is switching without animation.
  </p>

  <script>

    /*
     * INSIGHT (as required by assignment):
     *
     * 1. What the View Transitions API does:
     *    It lets the browser take a screenshot of the current page state (old view),
     *    then after a DOM update, animate the transition between old and new visually.
     *    This is done using document.startViewTransition(() => { ... }).
     *
     * 2. Why it improves UX compared to instant DOM changes:
     *    Without it, swapping content is abrupt and jarring — the user loses visual
     *    context of what just changed. With View Transitions, the user sees a smooth
     *    animation that makes the change feel natural, like flipping a page.
     *
     * 3. How view-transition-name works:
     *    Assigning view-transition-name: content-area in CSS tells the browser to
     *    specifically capture and animate THAT element, giving fine-grained control
     *    over which parts of the page animate and how.
     */

    // all the view HTML stored as strings
    const views = {

      home: `
        <div class="view-home">
          <h2>🏠 Welcome to My Portfolio</h2>
          <p>
            Hi! I am a final year CSE student. This page demonstrates the
            View Transitions API — a modern browser feature that animates
            content changes without any external library.
          </p>
          <div class="home-cards">
            <div class="home-card">
              <div class="icon">💻</div>
              <p>Web Development</p>
            </div>
            <div class="home-card">
              <div class="icon">🤖</div>
              <p>Machine Learning</p>
            </div>
            <div class="home-card">
              <div class="icon">📱</div>
              <p>Android Apps</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      `,

      about: `
        <div class="view-about">
          <h2>👤 About Me</h2>
          <p>
            I am currently in my final year of B.Tech in Computer Science &
            Engineering. I enjoy building projects and learning new web technologies.
          </p>
          <ul class="about-list">
            <li><span>🎓</span> B.Tech CSE — Final Year</li>
            <li><span>📍</span> Karnataka, India</li>
            <li><span>🛠️</span> Skills: HTML, CSS, JS, Python, React</li>
            <li><span>🎯</span> Goal: Full Stack Developer</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      `,

      contact: `
        <div class="view-contact">
          <h2>📬 Contact Me</h2>
          <p>
            Feel free to reach out for collaborations, project ideas, or just to say hello!
          </p>
          <div class="contact-info">
            <div class="contact-row"><span>📧</span> student@example.com</div>
            <div class="contact-row"><span>📞</span> +91 98765 43210</div>
            <div class="contact-row"><span>💼</span> linkedin.com/in/student</div>
            <div class="contact-row"><span>🐙</span> github.com/student</div>
          </div>
        </div>
      `

    };

    // track which view is currently shown
    let currentView = "home";

    // load the home view on page load
    document.getElementById("content-box").innerHTML = views["home"];

    function switchView(viewName, clickedBtn) {

      // do nothing if the user clicks the already active tab
      if (viewName === currentView) return;

      // update active tab button styling
      document.querySelectorAll(".tab-btn").forEach(btn => btn.classList.remove("active"));
      clickedBtn.classList.add("active");

      // check if View Transitions API is supported by this browser
      if (!document.startViewTransition) {

        // fallback for older browsers — just swap content directly
        document.getElementById("content-box").innerHTML = views[viewName];
        document.getElementById("fallback-notice").style.display = "block";
        currentView = viewName;
        return;
      }

      // USE THE VIEW TRANSITIONS API
      // startViewTransition() captures the current screen, runs our DOM update,
      // then animates between the old screenshot and the new content smoothly.
      document.startViewTransition(() => {
        document.getElementById("content-box").innerHTML = views[viewName];
        currentView = viewName;
      });

    }

  </script>

</body>
</html>

3.	Shopping Cart Calculator Essentials: Local Storage & Responsive UI
Assignment :  
Assignment - Shopping Cart Calculator
🎯 Objective
Build a simple Shopping Cart Calculator using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript that allows users to add products, calculate totals, and retain cart data using localStorage.
Task: Build a Simple Shopping Cart
Create a small shopping cart page with the following features:
1️⃣ Product Input
•	Product Name
•	Price
•	Quantity
•	An “Add to Cart” button
2️⃣ Cart Display
•	Show added products in a list/table
•	Display:
•	Product name
•	Price
•	Quantity
•	Total price per item
3️⃣ Cart Calculation
•	Calculate and display:
•	Total number of items
•	Grand total price
4️⃣ Local Storage (Mandatory)
•	Save cart data using localStorage
•	Cart should persist after page refresh
5️⃣ Responsive UI (Basic)
•	Layout should work on mobile and desktop
•	Use simple CSS (flexbox or basic layout is enough)
________________________________________
📤 Submission Instructions
•	Submit your HTML, CSS, and JavaScript code in the text boxTask: Build a Simple Shopping Cart
Create a small shopping cart page with the following features:
1️⃣ Product Input
•	Product Name
•	Price
•	Quantity
•	An “Add to Cart” button
2️⃣ Cart Display
•	Show added products in a list/table
•	Display:
•	Product name
•	Price
•	Quantity
•	Total price per item
3️⃣ Cart Calculation
•	Calculate and display:
•	Total number of items
•	Grand total price
4️⃣ Local Storage (Mandatory)
•	Save cart data using localStorage
•	Cart should persist after page refresh
5️⃣ Responsive UI (Basic)
•	Layout should work on mobile and desktop
•	Use simple CSS (flexbox or basic layout is enough)
________________________________________
📤 Submission Instructions
•	Submit your HTML, CSS, and JavaScript code in the text box



Code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"/>
  <title>Shopping Cart Calculator</title>
  <style>

    /* basic reset */
    * {
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    body {
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
      background-color: #f0f4f8;
      color: #333;
      padding: 30px 16px;
    }

    h1 {
      text-align: center;
      font-size: 1.7rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 6px;
    }

    .subtitle {
      text-align: center;
      font-size: 0.88rem;
      color: #888;
      margin-bottom: 28px;
    }

    /* main layout — two column on desktop */
    .layout {
      display: flex;
      gap: 22px;
      max-width: 960px;
      margin: 0 auto;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
    }

    /* ── INPUT PANEL ── */
    .input-panel {
      background: #fff;
      border-radius: 12px;
      box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0.09);
      padding: 26px 22px;
      flex: 1 1 280px;
    }

    .input-panel h2 {
      font-size: 1.05rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
      margin-bottom: 18px;
    }

    label {
      display: block;
      font-size: 0.82rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      color: #555;
      margin-bottom: 5px;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      letter-spacing: 0.4px;
    }

    input[type="text"],
    input[type="number"] {
      width: 100%;
      padding: 10px 12px;
      border: 1px solid #ccc;
      border-radius: 8px;
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      background: #f9f9f9;
      margin-bottom: 14px;
      outline: none;
      transition: border-color 0.2s;
    }

    input:focus {
      border-color: #1a73e8;
      background: #fff;
    }

    .btn-add {
      width: 100%;
      padding: 12px;
      background: #1a73e8;
      color: #fff;
      border: none;
      border-radius: 8px;
      font-size: 1rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      cursor: pointer;
      transition: background 0.3s, transform 0.2s;
    }

    .btn-add:hover {
      background: #1558b0;
      transform: scale(1.02);
    }

    /* error message */
    .error-msg {
      font-size: 0.82rem;
      color: #e53935;
      margin-top: 8px;
      display: none;
    }

    /* ── CART PANEL ── */
    .cart-panel {
      background: #fff;
      border-radius: 12px;
      box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0.09);
      padding: 26px 22px;
      flex: 2 1 400px;
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
      gap: 18px;
    }

    .cart-panel h2 {
      font-size: 1.05rem;
      color: #1a73e8;
    }

    /* cart table */
    .cart-table {
      width: 100%;
      border-collapse: collapse;
      font-size: 0.88rem;
    }

    .cart-table th {
      background: #e8f0fe;
      color: #1a73e8;
      padding: 10px 10px;
      text-align: left;
      font-size: 0.78rem;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      letter-spacing: 0.4px;
    }

    .cart-table td {
      padding: 10px 10px;
      border-bottom: 1px solid #f0f0f0;
      color: #444;
      vertical-align: middle;
    }

    .cart-table tr:last-child td {
      border-bottom: none;
    }

    .cart-table tr:hover td {
      background: #fafafa;
    }

    /* item total cell */
    .item-total {
      font-weight: bold;
      color: #27ae60;
    }

    /* delete button inside table */
    .btn-delete {
      background: #fdecea;

      cursor: pointer;
      transition: background 0.2s;
    }

    .btn-delete:hover {
      background: #e53935;
      color: #fff;
    }

    /* empty cart message */
    .empty-msg {
      text-align: center;

      padding: 20px 0;
    }

    /* ── SUMMARY BAR ── */
    .summary {
      background: #f0f4f8;
      border-radius: 10px;

      display: flex;
      justify-content: space-between;
      align-items: center;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 12px;
    }

    .summary-item {
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
      gap: 2px;
    }

    .summary-label {
      font-size: 0.75rem;
      color: #888;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      letter-spacing: 0.4px;
    }

    .summary-value {
      font-size: 1.2rem;
      font-weight: bold;
      color: #1a73e8;
    }

    .grand-total .summary-value {
      color: #27ae60;
      font-size: 1.4rem;
    }

    /* clear cart button */
    .btn-clear {
      background: none;

      border-radius: 8px;

      font-weight: bold;
      cursor: pointer;
      transition: background 0.2s, color 0.2s;
    }

    .btn-clear:hover {
      background: #e53935;
      color: #fff;
    }

    /* localStorage badge */
    .storage-badge {
      display: inline-flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 5px;
      font-size: 0.72rem;

      background: #eafaf1;
      border-radius: 20px;
      padding: 3px 10px;
      font-weight: bold;
    }

    /* responsive */
    @media (max-width: 600px) {
      .layout { flex-direction: column; }

      .cart-table th:nth-child(3),
      .cart-table td:nth-child(3) { display: none; } /* hide qty col on very small */

      h1 { font-size: 1.4rem; }
    }

  </style>
</head>
<body>

  <h1>🛒 Shopping Cart Calculator</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">Add products, track totals — data saved automatically</p>

  <div class="layout">

    <!-- INPUT PANEL -->
    <div class="input-panel">
      <h2>➕ Add Product</h2>

      <label for="productName">Product Name</label>
      <input type="text" id="productName" placeholder="e.g. Notebook" />

      <label for="productPrice">Price (₹)</label>
      <input type="number" id="productPrice" placeholder="e.g. 120" min="0" />

      <label for="productQty">Quantity</label>
      <input type="number" id="productQty" placeholder="e.g. 2" min="1" value="1" />

      <button class="btn-add" onclick="addToCart()">Add to Cart</button>
      <p class="error-msg" id="errorMsg">⚠️ Please fill in all fields correctly.</p>
    </div>

    <!-- CART PANEL -->
    <div class="cart-panel">
      <div style="display:flex; justify-content:space-between; align-items:center; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:8px;">
        <h2>🧾 Cart Items</h2>
        <span class="storage-badge">💾 Auto-saved</span>
      </div>

      <!-- cart table -->
      <div style="overflow-x:auto;">
        <table class="cart-table">
          <thead>
            <tr>
              <th>#</th>
              <th>Product</th>
              <th>Qty</th>
              <th>Price</th>
              <th>Total</th>
              <th></th>
            </tr>
          </thead>
          <tbody id="cartBody">
            <!-- rows injected by JS -->
          </tbody>
        </table>
        <p class="empty-msg" id="emptyMsg">Your cart is empty. Add some products!</p>
      </div>

      <!-- summary bar -->
      <div class="summary">
        <div class="summary-item">
          <span class="summary-label">Total Items</span>
          <span class="summary-value" id="totalItems">0</span>
        </div>
        <div class="summary-item">
          <span class="summary-label">Products</span>
          <span class="summary-value" id="totalProducts">0</span>
        </div>
        <div class="summary-item grand-total">
          <span class="summary-label">Grand Total</span>
          <span class="summary-value" id="grandTotal">₹0.00</span>
        </div>
        <button class="btn-clear" onclick="clearCart()">🗑 Clear All</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <script>

    // ── load cart from localStorage on page load ──
    let cart = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("shopping-cart")) || [];

    // render whatever is already saved
    renderCart();

    // ── ADD TO CART ──
    function addToCart() {
      const nameInput  = document.getElementById("productName");
      const priceInput = document.getElementById("productPrice");
      const qtyInput   = document.getElementById("productQty");
      const errorMsg   = document.getElementById("errorMsg");

      const name  = nameInput.value.trim();
      const price = parseFloat(priceInput.value);
      const qty   = parseInt(qtyInput.value);

      // validate inputs
      if (!name || isNaN(price) || price < 0 || isNaN(qty) || qty < 1) {
        errorMsg.style.display = "block";
        return;
      }

      errorMsg.style.display = "none";

      // check if same product already in cart — increase qty instead
      const existing = cart.find(item => item.name.toLowerCase() === name.toLowerCase());
      if (existing) {
        existing.qty   += qty;
        existing.total  = existing.price * existing.qty;
      } else {
        cart.push({
          id:    Date.now(),
          name,
          price,
          qty,
          total: price * qty
        });
      }

      // save to localStorage
      localStorage.setItem("shopping-cart", JSON.stringify(cart));

      // clear inputs
      nameInput.value  = "";
      priceInput.value = "";
      qtyInput.value   = "1";
      nameInput.focus();

      renderCart();
    }

    // ── DELETE ONE ITEM ──
    function deleteItem(id) {
      cart = cart.filter(item => item.id !== id);
      localStorage.setItem("shopping-cart", JSON.stringify(cart));
      renderCart();
    }

    // ── CLEAR ENTIRE CART ──
    function clearCart() {
      if (cart.length === 0) return;
      if (confirm("Are you sure you want to clear the entire cart?")) {
        cart = [];
        localStorage.removeItem("shopping-cart");
        renderCart();
      }
    }

    // ── RENDER CART TABLE ──
    function renderCart() {
      const tbody      = document.getElementById("cartBody");
      const emptyMsg   = document.getElementById("emptyMsg");
      const totalItems = document.getElementById("totalItems");
      const totalProds = document.getElementById("totalProducts");
      const grandTotal = document.getElementById("grandTotal");

      tbody.innerHTML = "";

      if (cart.length === 0) {
        emptyMsg.style.display = "block";
      } else {
        emptyMsg.style.display = "none";

        cart.forEach((item, index) => {
          const row = document.createElement("tr");
          row.innerHTML = `
            <td>${index + 1}</td>
            <td>${item.name}</td>
            <td>${item.qty}</td>
            <td>₹${item.price.toFixed(2)}</td>
            <td class="item-total">₹${item.total.toFixed(2)}</td>
            <td>
              <button class="btn-delete" onclick="deleteItem(${item.id})">✕</button>
            </td>
          `;
          tbody.appendChild(row);
        });
      }

      // update summary
      const sumQty   = cart.reduce((acc, item) => acc + item.qty, 0);
      const sumTotal = cart.reduce((acc, item) => acc + item.total, 0);

      totalItems.textContent = sumQty;
      totalProds.textContent = cart.length;
      grandTotal.textContent = "₹" + sumTotal.toFixed(2);
    }

    // ── allow Enter key to add product ──
    document.addEventListener("keydown", function(e) {
      if (e.key === "Enter") addToCart();
    });

  </script>

</body>
</html>

4.	URL SearchParams Essentials: Modern JS Mini Workshop
Assignment :  
Assignment: URLSearchParams Essentials – Modern JavaScript
🎯 Objective
Learn how to use the URLSearchParams API in JavaScript to:
•	Read query parameters from a URL
•	Add, update, and remove parameters
•	Use query params to control page behavior
🛠️ Task Description
Scenario
You are building a simple webpage that reads data from the URL query string.
Example URL:
https://example.com/?name=Rahul&city=Delhi&theme=dark
________________________________________
1️⃣ Read URL Parameters (Mandatory)
Using JavaScript:
•	Extract the following parameters from the URL:
•	name
•	city
•	theme
•	Display them clearly on the page or in the console
________________________________________
2️⃣ Modify URL Parameters (Mandatory)
Using URLSearchParams:
•	Add a new parameter course=javascript
•	Update the theme value
•	Remove one existing parameter
________________________________________
3️⃣ Dynamic Usage (Mandatory)
Use at least one parameter to change behavior, for example:
•	Apply light/dark theme
•	Display a personalized greeting using the name
•	Conditionally show content based on city
________________________________________
4️⃣ Explanation (Mandatory)
Write 2–3 lines explaining:
•	What URLSearchParams is
•	One real-world use case



Code:
